quick_skim.title: MetaboliSim: a Python implementation of the Mader model for dynamic and steady-state simulation of muscular energy metabolism
quick_skim.abstract: The Mader model is the most widely used mathematical framework for muscular energy metabolism in German-language sport science, underpinning lactate diagnostics, maximal lactate steady state (MLSS) estimation and training prescription. Despite decades of use, neither its dynamic ODE formulation nor its steady-state equations have been available as open code, leaving results based on the model impossible to reproduce independently. We close this gap with MetaboliSim, an open-source Python implementation of both formulations: a dynamic model that integrates the five-variable ODE system (phosphate potential, $\dot{V}\mathrm{O}_2$, muscle and blood lactate, and glycogen) with a fourth-order Runge-Kutta scheme, and a steady-state model that computes MLSS power and the lactate-power relationship in one- and two-compartment variants. We verified implementation correctness against published reference values and assessed physiological plausibility across constant-load, step-test, sprint and running protocols. The implementation reproduces the published reference output within stated tolerances and remains numerically stable throughout (halving the time step changes blood lactate by less than 0.01 mmol/L), with both formulations yielding congruent MLSS estimates. Key physiological behaviour ($\dot{V}\mathrm{O}_2$ on-kinetics, lactate accumulation, PCr dynamics and the sub/supra-MLSS separation) emerges directly from the model equations without protocol-specific tuning, and a sensitivity analysis shows MLSS power varying approximately linearly with $\dot{V}\mathrm{O}_{2\max}$ and nonlinearly with $\dot{V}\mathrm{La}_{\max}$. As the first openly available implementation of the complete Mader model (AGPL-3.0), MetaboliSim lets independent groups reproduce, verify and build on published model-based results. Source code: https://codeberg.org/3phos/metabolisim; Platform: https://metabolisim.org
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: provides open-source simulation of muscular energy metabolism, directly relevant to exercise-induced metabolic changes
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提供肌肉能量代谢的开源模拟，直接与运动诱导的代谢变化相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提供肌肉能量代谢的开源模拟，直接与运动诱导的代谢变化相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提供肌肉能量代谢的开源模拟，直接与运动诱导的代谢变化相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MetaboliSim implements the Mader model for dynamic and steady-state simulation of muscular energy metabolism, enabling reproducible lactate diagnostics and training prescription. This tool can be adapted to simulate metabolic changes in skin flaps under exercise conditions.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: MetaboliSim开源实现了Mader模型，用于动态和稳态模拟肌肉能量代谢，包括磷酸势、耗氧量、乳酸等变量。该工具弥补了原模型代码缺失的问题，支持运动科学中的乳酸诊断和训练处方。尽管针对肌肉，但其核心代谢模拟框架可扩展至皮肤组织，用于研究运动训练对皮瓣代谢的影响，从而间接评估皮瓣存活。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: MetaboliSim开源实现了Mader模型，用于动态和稳态模拟肌肉能量代谢，包括磷酸势、耗氧量、乳酸等变量。该工具弥补了原模型代码缺失的问题，支持运动科学中的乳酸诊断和训练处方。尽管针对肌肉，但其核心代谢模拟框架可扩展至皮肤组织，用于研究运动训练对皮瓣代谢的影响，从而间接评估皮瓣存活。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: metabolic changes during exercise and flap survival
